_readme: THE single registry of server config surfaces. One row per file/folder. Four consumers
read THIS file so a config is declared in exactly one place:
- Api/deploy/Deploy-Api.ps1        -> the web read/write allowlist (rows with web != 'none')
- Deploy-DayZServer.ps1            -> seed-if-missing on a fresh box (rows with a 'seed')
- Sync-*/Pull-Configs.ps1          -> which box files are mirrored back (the 'mirror' tag)
- Confirm-LiveConfigs.ps1             -> which files to parse-check (the 'check' tag)
Add a config = add one row here. STRICT JSON (no comments/trailing commas) so every
consumer's plain JSON parser reads it.
Fields (file row): name, box (ServerDir-relative path on the server), scope, seed, mirror,
web, writable, check, plus two OPTIONAL display fields - label + group. label = the human
name shown in the UI; it defaults to name, and name STAYS the API key (configs/get?name=),
so a label never breaks a caller. group = the sidebar section the row lists under; it
defaults to 'General'. Both ride through CONFIG_MAP -> config-list to the editor. Folder
row: group, dir, subfolders, scope, web:'browse'.
scope    - 'shared' = one file applied to EVERY map (edit once); 'map:<mission>' = belongs
to that mission only. AI_Bandits shared templates live in common/ and apply to all
maps; per-map placements live in maps/<mission>/. The overrides DOCUMENT is one
shared file whose OWN schema layers common (all missions) vs per-mission patches.
seed     - repo-root-relative source copied to the box ONLY when missing (fresh box / disaster
recovery); null = box-generated, never seeded from the repo. CODE files (mods.conf,
scripts, units) are shipped by Deploy-DayZServer's own $items, NOT seeded here.
mirror   - which pull backs the box copy up: 'overrides' | 'spawns' | 'defaults' | 'live' | null.
'defaults' = the file's frozen <stem>.defaults<ext> baseline is glob-pulled by
Sync-ConfigDefaults (the file itself is rebuilt from default+patches each boot).
'live' (FOLDER rows, 2026-07-21) = the LIVE files themselves are pulled into
config-mirror/ by Pull-Configs and seeded back seed-if-missing by the deploy.
For box-born content that no seed and no other pull covers - mission
expansion/settings being the case that exposed the gap: a fresh box (and every
staging VM) had NO copy of the live mission config at all, so staging could not
reproduce prod and nothing noticed when a mission file lost 17 patrols.
Add 'mirror':'live' (+ optional 'mirrorGlob', default *.json) to a folder row
and it is pulled, versioned and seeded - no new script, no code change.
'live' on a FILE row (2026-07-23, the web-edited tuning pair) pulls the box copy
back INTO the row's 'seed' path (map-points model: seed = latest mirror, one
location) - NOT into config-mirror/, which would be a second mirror of one path.
The file must pass the row's 'check' parse before it may enter the repo.
web      - how the UI exposes it: patch (override editor) | file (whole-file) | store (Map
editor) | view (read-only) | browse (folder) | types (CE types-table editor) |
none (not shown; e.g. seed-only).
view is ENFORCED: it renders a read-only flag through config-list, and the editor
locks the row (no edit, no save) - not just a naming convention. Use it for files
the box owns as reference/code (e.g. custom-ce/ types, mods.conf, messages.xml).
types (2026-07-23) = a CE types XML the web EDITS as a table of <type> entries
(the Expansion tuning pair). Box-owned like the overrides document: dayz-ctl's
types-write is the ONLY writer, gated to kind 'types' rows; whole-file edit stays
locked. Which BASE file a tuning row overlays is hardcoded in editor.js
(TYPES_BASE) - promote it to a registry field if types surfaces multiply.
writable - whole-file write verb (box-owned operational lists only: bans/whitelist).
check    - Confirm-LiveConfigs parse assertion: json | xml | none.
mod      - (optional) the mods.conf @folder this surface belongs to. If that mod is DISABLED
(commented out in deploy/mods.conf), the API renders the surface's box path into
dayz-ctl's config-disabled list and the web editor DROPS the row entirely - a mod
turned off must not surface its config files or override-patch targets. The box-owned
override patches stay intact (reversible); re-enable the mod + redeploy to restore.
Cross-referenced at Api DEPLOY time (Deploy-Api reads mods.conf beside this registry).
generated - relpaths (mission '*' glob allowed) of files a prestart COMPILER writes: they are
artifacts, never inputs. The API renders these into dayz-ctl; the web editor shows any match
READ-ONLY (no edit, no save) and override-write REFUSES a patch that targets one - so a value
that is auto-generated can never be hand-edited into a silent loss. Edit the SOURCE (map-points
via the Map tab, the common templates, the frozen base), never the output. Add a compiler
output = add one line here.
DRAFTS (2026-07-21): a builder that has no business owning a hand-authored file writes a
<name>.draft.json beside it instead. The DRAFT is the generated file (listed here, read-only);
the live file it drafts is NOT generated and stays fully editable. Expansion AIPatrolSettings
.json / AILocationSettings.json work this way - Build-AIPatrols/Build-AILocations compose the
draft every boot so you can see what map-points WOULD produce, and never touch the live file.
